explanatory_strength.rationale: Round-two review does not weaken the mechanism: AWS scale and margin (Q1 2026 +28%, 37.7% segment OI), retail logistics density, and high-margin ads/silicon still form a coherent reinvestment story with explicit falsifiers. The distinguishing test remains whether AI capex converts to normalized owner cash; that test is stated, not hand-waved.

evidence_sufficiency.rationale: The targeted research loop confirmed the contract proof mis-specifies owner earnings at $7.7B while the cited operating-cash-flow tag is $148.5B TTM, so the reinvestment DCF cannot anchor pricing. It also marked the capex-bridge question unresolved: ~$90B sustainable capex is still [Assumption], management_facts has zero documents, and filing_facts_2026-07-20 parsed a 2003 annual report. Core SEC operating facts reconcile, but decision-grade normalized owner cash and incremental AI return on capital remain unverified.

downside_control.rationale: Balance-sheet liquidity ($86.8B cash, $148.5B TTM operating cash flow) limits insolvency risk and provides a real economic floor. Downside is not structurally protected at ~$250: trailing FCF is ~$1.2B, productive-asset capex ($151.0B) nearly equals OCF, and equity remains exposed to prolonged AI build-out and multiple compression on ~46x normalized owner cash.

return_vs_alternatives.rationale: Using the filing-grounded Lawrence bridge (~$5.35/sh normalized owner cash), total synthesis is 3.68% per year at ~$247-250; Lawrence gate is 1.9%; bull is 7.2%; AI sensitivity at $9/sh is 16.2% but unproven. None clears the Buffett/Weschler 12% hurdle without the unresolved capex-normalization path. Contract base value $11.64/sh at spot implies deeply negative annualized returns if taken literally and is disqualified by the confirmed input error.

claims.claim: Amazon remains a widening-scale compounder with stable moat characteristics (AWS lock-in, Prime, logistics, custom silicon) squarely within the quality_reinvestment power zone.

The research loop confirmed the contract owner_earnings_reinvestment_dcf proof row is mis-specified ($7.695B labeled normalized owner earnings vs $148.5B TTM operating cash flow); committee pricing must use the Lawrence normalized bridge (~$58.5B / ~$5.35 per share) until the proof input is corrected.
At ~$250 per share, evidenced return paths (1.9%-7.2%) fail the 12% owner-earnings reinvestment bar; the unresolved capex-to-normalized-FCF bridge blocks capital approval despite franchise quality.

strongest_counter_explanation: The AI infrastructure capex cycle is temporary capital allocation, not permanent value destruction: AWS re-acceleration, ads scale above $70B TTM, and custom silicon will lift normalized owner earnings toward $9+ per share within three to four years, making today's price fair for a widening-moat compounder priced on future distributable cash.
most_important_missing_fact: Management-disclosed sustainable capex run rate and explicit timeline for free cash flow per share normalization after the AI data-center build cycle, including observable incremental after-tax return on AI and datacenter capital and whether ~$90B mid-cycle capex is achievable without sacrificing AWS share.
falsifiers: Two consecutive quarters with operating cash flow growth below 5% year over year while productive-asset capex stays above $140B.
AWS revenue growth decelerates below 15% with segment operating margin falling below 32% for two consecutive quarters.
Normalized owner cash per share fails to exceed $6 by 2028 while share count dilution exceeds 2% per year.
specialist_findings: Within power zone (compounder, stable moat, partial dhando). Round-two audit: research_response answered that contract owner_earnings ($7.695B) contradicts its cited OCF locator ($148.5B TTM); reinvestment DCF base value_per_share ($11.64) and annualized returns (-41% to -29% at $249.99) are not decision inputs. Research loop left the capex bridge from ~$151B spot to ~$90B sustainable run rate unresolved; incremental ROIC bounds (12-25%) remain judgment without a primary capital ledger. No comparable set attached. Single additive component (entire_security) avoids overlap double-count; ads and AI backlog embedded_in_segment with explicit zero/milestone treatments. Overlap controls pass; capital requirement risk is the capex-OCF gap (~$61B reserve), not balance-sheet distress.
